reasoning: Based on the FRED historical data, the 3-month Treasury yield closed at 4.23 on August 29, 2025, which serves as our baseline. Over the short horizon to Sept 7, no FOMC meeting will take place, and ordinary daily fluctuations present a reasonably high chance (0.35, aligning with the FRED statistical projection) that the yield bounces slightly above the recent low of 4.23. For Sept 30, the probability decreases significantly (to 0.25), as markets broadly anticipate (~90% probability) the Federal Reserve will enact at least a 25 basis point rate cut at their mid-September meeting. This would lower the federal funds target to 4.00-4.25%, pulling the 3-month yield down to approximately 4.10%. For the later dates (Nov 2025 and Feb 2026), there is greater uncertainty surrounding inflation, economic growth, and the continued pace of Fed rate cuts; therefore, adhering to the instruction to increase uncertainty over time, the probabilities gradually revert toward 0.5 (0.35 and 0.45, respectively).
